In this lesson, we're going to talk about the key elements of your transaction maximization pages. That's what goes on these different pages. Now everything that we're going to talk about in this lesson applies to the reverse add-on offer page, the soft add-on offer page, and the mid-tier add-on offer page. It does not apply to the thank you gift offer page, which we're going to talk about in the next lesson. Let's go through the different key elements of these pages. The first is that all of your transaction maximization pages must maintain a continuity of branding. That just means that they need to look and feel the same. They need to have the same coloring, the same logo, the same header if possible. They need to look and feel like they are part of a sequence, not as if they are disconnected pages that don't relate to each other. As well, your transaction maximization pages need to convey that the transaction is still in progress. A mistake that a lot of marketers make, even with the general type of upsell sequence that you see, is after the initial offer is conveying to the customer that the transaction is done. They say, hey, thank you for your order. In the meantime, take a look at this. That's a mistake that I made for a long time. You want to convey that the transaction is still in progress, that the transaction is still occurring, that they are still in the middle of the ordering process. Even though when they submit the order form for the customer captivation offer, we've got their credit card, we've processed their order. In essence, the order is complete. We want to convey to the customer that they are still actually in the process of completing their order. The next element is that these pages acknowledge a continuance of order, meaning it acknowledges their previous step. Even though these pages need to convey that the transaction is still in progress, it also needs to acknowledge that they placed their order for the prior product. Next element is that these pages display a checkout progress status bar. I'll show you an example of what this looks like in just a minute. Next is that the transaction maximization page, especially the reverse add-on offer page, that it tells the customer that their product details will be sent to them via email in 10 minutes. This is critical because what this does is convey to the customer that there's no reason for them to run off, check their email to get started with your product. We want to give them a reason to be okay with staying on this page and looking at the offers that you're going to be presenting through the transaction maximization sequence. By telling a customer that their product details will be sent to them by email in 10 minutes, again, it gives them the time to consume your marketing message to make a decision about these offers. Next element is that it highlights a special offer. In other words, your transaction maximization offers need to be special offers. These should not be offers that a customer can get anywhere else at any other time. You need to convey to them that this is a special offer. In some cases, you want to convey that this is a special offer, that they will only see one time right now during the ordering process. Then finally, the transaction maximization pages are presented with either a video sales letter or a long form sales letter or the reverse add on and the soft add on are done with short form sales letter with a call to action area. Let me show you an example of all of these elements at play on a transaction maximization page. What you're looking at right here is you're looking at a screenshot of the top of an order form for one of our products. What you're looking at is the first add on offer right here. They're going from this to right here. The first thing that you'll notice is that you can see continuity of branding. You can see the logo over here as well as the logo over here. They ordered the 26 advanced marketing funnel conversion tactics product over here. You see the logo, if you will, the header for that product right here. This is conveying this continuity of branding. Of course, you see which we're going to cover the progress bar. The point is I want you to see the coloring, the look and feel, the logo. It's all a continuity of branding. You could see that it conveys that the transaction is still in progress because it says right here, step number two, your order for the 26 advanced marketing funnel conversion tactics is almost complete. Even though they ordered or they placed their order for that product on the order form and their credit card was charged and the order was processed, again, we want to convey that the transaction is still in progress, that they're not done yet. Next, it acknowledges again a continuance of the order. Look, we're acknowledging the fact that they placed an order for this particular product. We're saying your order, we're acknowledging the fact that they placed an order for that product. Next, it displays the checkout progress status. The one thing that you will notice when you look at this is that this progress bar is a little different from the one that was on the order form. Let's actually go back for a second. You can see over here that the progress bar on the order form just has checkout order confirmation instant access. Then on the add-on offer, it's got this extra section, which is product options. The reason why you don't want this product options on the order form is because it immediately conveys to customers that they are going to be presented with a bunch of additional offers. Again, most customers or most prospects will immediately think, well, maybe this isn't complete then if there are other options that I'm going to be presented with. The progress bar on the order form does not have product options. Let's go back to this page again, displays the progress status. Next, it highlights that it's a special offer. You can see that it blatantly says, here's a special discounted offer. Now, the one thing that's actually missing from this page, and I mentioned it earlier, is it's missing the little blurb about the fact that their initial details will be sent to them in 10 minutes. The point is that you can see all of those other elements at play here on this one add-on offer. That is the key elements of your transaction maximization pages. If you use SAM cart, you'll be building out those different pages in SAM cart, and you can put all of these elements into each one of those pages with relative ease.
